title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every forest $H$, there is a function $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for every $H$-free graph $G$.
context_text: The Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture asserts that the class of $H$-free graphs is $\chi$-bounded whenever $H$ is a forest (the necessary condition, since triangle-free graphs can have arbitrarily large chromatic number by Erdős's construction). It remains open in general, though it has been verified for several restricted families of trees.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every double star $H$ the bounding function $f$ in the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture can be chosen to be a polynomial, i.e., $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ with $f$ a polynomial for every $H$-free graph $G$.
notes: Conjecture is labeled 1.1 in the paper and attributed to [8] (Gyárfás) and [20] (Sumner); Sumner's independent formulation dates to 1981, Gyárfás's to 1987.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. III. Excluding a double star

title: Esperet's Polynomial χ-Boundedness Conjecture
statement_text: For every $\chi$-bounded class $\mathcal{C}$, the bounding function $f$ (satisfying $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for all $G \in \mathcal{C}$) can be chosen to be a polynomial.
context_text: Esperet's conjecture is a bold strengthening: even though proofs of $\chi$-boundedness typically use iterated Ramsey arguments yielding rapidly growing functions, Esperet conjectured that a polynomial always suffices. In particular, it implies that the function $f$ in the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture can always be taken to be a polynomial.
paper_contribution: Proves Esperet's strengthening of the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture for all double stars $H$, and more generally for all trees that do not contain $P_5$ as an induced subgraph.
notes: Stated in prose without a labelled theorem environment; cited as [7] in the paper. The remaining open case highlighted is $H = P_5$.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. III. Excluding a double star
